title: Airlock Contract
description: Schema for an Airlock contract: a self-declared, machine-readable disclosure of what a self-deployed business agent does, who it serves, and on what terms, packaged so other businesses' AI agents can discover and integrate without prior coordination. v0.4 reframes the schema around buyer-facing questions (category, region, compliance, auth_model, pricing) and removes the v0.3 harness-internal blocks. See CONTEXT.md, docs/adr/0006, and docs/taxonomies.md.

airlock.description: Airlock spec version. v0.4 (and v0.4.x) accepted; v0.5 reserved for the upcoming signed-Agent-Card release. v0.1/v0.2/v0.3 are rejected (see docs/migration-v03-to-v04.md).

compliance.description: BINDING. Certifications and attestations the publisher claims. Each entry carries a status (certified | self_attested | in_progress) so consumers can weigh the claim.

tags.description: BINDING (open vocabulary). Free-form keywords the registry indexes alongside the curated category fields.

schemas.description: Reusable type definitions referenced by skills via $ref.

skills.description: BINDING. The set of skills this agent exposes to consumer agents. Each skill is one named external interaction.

authority.description: BINDING. Declarative rules describing what the agent will do unilaterally vs. refuse vs. escalate. Each rule self-classifies its binding_class and carries an optional human-readable summary + keywords for indexing.

instant_failures.description: BINDING. Conditions that reject the request on sight, before any work. Always produce PROMISE codes.

sla.description: BINDING. Per-skill response-time commitments. Keys are skill ids.

A2AInfo.description: INFORMATIONAL (ADR 0004/0007). Optional bridge hints for the derived A2A Agent Card. All fields are optional; sensible defaults derive from existing Airlock fields.

endpoint_url.description: Where the sandbox / runtime accepts A2A JSON-RPC. Defaults to <agent.homepage>/a2a if absent.

documentation_url.description: Where a developer reads docs. Defaults to agent.homepage.

streaming.description: Whether the agent responds to SendStreamingMessage. v0.4.1 MVP: always false.

push_notifications.description: Whether the agent supports webhook callbacks. v0.4.1 MVP: always false.

state_transition_history.description: Whether the agent persists task transition history. v0.4.1 MVP: always false.

default_input_modes.description: Default media types accepted by skills. Defaults to ['application/json'].

default_output_modes.description: Default media types emitted by skills. Defaults to ['application/json'].

name.description: Stable identifier for the agent. Recommend kebab-case.

version.description: Contract version. SemVer. Major bumps require a deprecation block on the prior major.

description.description: Human and LLM-readable summary of what the agent does and who it serves. The most important single field for indexing relevance.

channels.description: Channels this contract is served over. v1 ships only the http adapter.

homepage.description: Publisher's landing page or product URL.

Category.description: BINDING. The most important indexing surface — a registry can pre-filter every buyer query on these two fields. Both are required; both use curated closed vocabularies (see docs/taxonomies.md).

subcategory.description: Optional free-form refinement under the chosen industry (e.g. 'logistics:cold-chain'). Indexed; not part of the closed vocabulary.

Region.description: BINDING. Geographic posture. data_residency describes where the agent's own data lives; serves_regions describes which regions consumers may successfully transact from.

status.description: certified = third-party attested; self_attested = publisher claim only; in_progress = pursuing certification.

attestation_url.description: Optional pointer to the audit report, certificate, or self-attestation document.

verified_at.description: Date the certification was issued or last renewed.

AuthModel.description: BINDING. How a consumer agent authenticates and how it gets enrolled.

enrollment.description: open = anyone may register a key; approval_required = manual review; invite_only = onboarded by the publisher; enterprise_only = contract precedes API access.

support_url.description: Where a consumer goes to enrol or ask for credentials.

Pricing.description: BINDING (model + unit). Pricing posture so buyers can pre-filter. Hard numbers live behind price_url because they change too fast to live in the contract.

unit.description: Optional billing unit when model is metered/usage_tiered.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code (e.g. USD, EUR). Optional.

price_url.description: INFORMATIONAL. Canonical commercial-terms link buyers follow for actual numbers. Kept out of the schema so it can change without bumping.

Permissions.description: BINDING. Data-access disclosure for the buyer's risk-management process. NOT a developer-facing fs/network/tool allowlist — that lived in v0.3 and is gone.

pii.description: How much personal information the agent ingests in normal operation.

retention.description: Duration data is retained after the interaction completes (e.g. '0s', '30d', '7y', 'indefinite').

third_party_sharing.description: Whether the agent shares ingested data with any third party.

Guardrails.description: BINDING. Categorical refusals at the agent level. refused_topics / refused_actions are free-form strings linted against the recommended vocabulary in docs/taxonomies.md.

refused_topics.description: Topic strings the agent will not engage with (e.g. 'financial_advice', 'medical_diagnosis').

refused_actions.description: Named actions the agent will not perform (e.g. 'commit_secrets', 'transfer_funds_to_new_payee').

required_authentication.description: If true, the agent refuses unauthenticated requests categorically.

TypeDef.description: A JSON-Schema-compatible type definition. Airlock embeds JSON Schema as the type system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the skill. Recommend snake_case.

input.description: JSON Schema for the input payload. Can be inline or a $ref into #/schemas.

output.description: JSON Schema for the output payload on success.

examples.description: Worked examples. Used by the renderer, the sandbox (example replay), and the conformance runner. The sandbox falls back to a schema-derived faker when no example matches (see ADR 0005).

ExpectedVerdict.description: What pre-flight should return for this example input. The conformance runner uses this as an assertion when the example's code is a PROMISE.

ref.description: Optional pointer to the rule expected to produce this verdict.

AuthorityRule.description: A rule targeting a specific skill. Carries optional summary + keywords so registries can index the *substance* of the rule, not just its expression source.

id.description: Stable identifier; referenced by `ref` in verdicts.

summary.description: BINDING (when present). Human-readable one-liner describing what the rule does in business terms. Renders as the rule's headline; indexed by the registry. Highly recommended.

keywords.description: Free-form indexing keywords (e.g. ['purchase_order', 'auto_accept', 'threshold_10000']). Registry indexes alongside the contract-level tags.

skill.description: Skill id this rule applies to.

field.description: Optional: the input field the rule operates on (for docs rendering).

binding_class.description: Whether this rule's verdicts are PROMISE (deterministic) or ESTIMATE (judgment). The validator lints: deterministic rules may not reference runtime state.

when.description: Expression evaluated against the input. Tiny safe expression language: comparisons, arithmetic, logical, field refs, abs/min/max/len, regex match.

InstantFailure.description: Conditions that reject on sight. Always PROMISE.

summary.description: BINDING (when present). One-liner describing the failure in business terms.

skill.description: Optional: scope this failure to a specific skill. Omit to apply to all skills.

when.description: Expression evaluated against the input (same tiny safe language as authority rules).

code.description: Must be a Phase 1 or Phase 2 code (identification or input validation).

Actions.description: Catalog of foreign actions this agent may take in real responses.

respond_within.description: Duration string (e.g. "5m", "24h", "3d"). Pre-flight to terminal lifecycle state.

on_breach.description: What happens if the SLA is breached.

states.description: Lifecycle states this agent uses. Subset of Phase 5 codes.

Deprecation.description: When set, this contract version is being phased out. Pre-flight against this version returns OUT_OF_SCOPE with the redirect in detail.

replaced_by_url.description: URL of the contract that replaces this one.

sunset.description: Date after which this contract is no longer honored.

Industry.description: Closed v0.4 industry vocabulary. Adding values requires an ADR. See docs/taxonomies.md.

Capability.description: Closed v0.4 capability vocabulary. Describes what kind of work the agent does, irrespective of industry.

RegionCode.description: Coarse geographic + regulatory region. Closed vocabulary; finer-grained sub-regions can live in tags.

ComplianceStandard.description: Closed v0.4 set of recognised certification standards.

AuthMethod.description: Closed v0.4 set of authentication methods.

PricingUnit.description: Closed v0.4 set of billing units.

DataClass.description: Closed v0.4 set of data sensitivity classes the agent ingests or produces.

StatusCode.description: All Airlock status codes across the 6 phases. Phases 1–3 are PROMISE; phase 4 is ESTIMATE; phases 5–6 are real-response only.

ActionCode.description: Phase 6 action codes — what the agent actually did in a real response.
